summary: Integrated block_selector.py into process.py pipeline with full selection logging and reasoning. Tested against 5 real meetings with excellent results.

integration_details.fallback: --legacy flag reverts to static block lists
integration_details.manifest_logging: block_selection object with method, recipe, always/conditional/triggered arrays, and per-block reasoning

test_results.notes: Rich business meeting - correctly identified deliverables, relationship trajectory, business context
Strategic discussion - correctly identified decision rationale and strategic intelligence
Personal/relationship focus - correctly prioritized relationship trajectory over business context
Brief meeting - correctly generated only always blocks
Group intro meeting - correctly skipped all conditionals with clear reasoning

design_decisions: Integration via import: process.py imports select_blocks() from block_selector.py for clean separation
Manifest logging: Full selection metadata including recipe, arrays, and reasoning stored in manifest.block_selection
Legacy mode: --legacy flag preserves original static selection for comparison/debugging
Graceful fallback: If selector import fails, falls back to legacy selection with warning
Reasoning transparency: All conditional decisions (selected AND skipped) logged with explanations
observations: Selection logic is appropriately selective - not over-generating conditional blocks
Reasoning quality is excellent - explains WHY blocks were skipped, not just that they were
Trigger detection working correctly - no false positives for B07/B14 verbal cues
Recipe selection working - all external meetings correctly use external_standard
Processing time: 30-180 seconds per meeting depending on transcript length
prompt_tuning_notes: No prompt tuning needed for initial tests - selection logic and reasoning are producing high-quality results. V should review actual block generation quality in production to identify any tuning needs.
notes_for_orchestrator: Integration complete and tested. V approval still pending for production rollout. Process.py now has two modes: smart selector (default) and legacy (--legacy flag). The manifest.block_selection field provides full transparency into selection decisions for debugging and future analysis.
